# Architecture — test-memory
<!-- Generated by memory-core on 2026-05-11 -->

**Type:** backend | **Language:** Typescript

---
## Backend — NestJS

NestJS with feature modules, DI, guards, pipes, and repository pattern

### Rules
1. Feature modules are self-contained — each has its own module, controller, service, DTOs, and entities
2. Controllers are thin — validate HTTP input, delegate to service, return response. Zero business logic.
3. Services own all business logic — never import controllers or HTTP-specific classes
4. Use class-validator and class-transformer with ValidationPipe for all incoming DTOs
5. Repository pattern — services depend on repository interfaces, never directly on TypeORM/Prisma
6. Guards handle all authentication and authorization — never check tokens inside controllers or services
7. Use exception filters for all error handling — never return raw errors or catch exceptions in controllers
8. [object Object]
9. ConfigModule with Joi/zod validation for all environment variables at startup
10. Each module uses forwardRef() only as a last resort — restructure to remove circular dependencies
11. Global pipes, guards, and filters are registered in main.ts — never duplicated per-controller
12. Use custom decorators to extract common parameter logic from controllers

### Folder Structure
```
src/modules/{feature}//
src/modules/{feature}/{feature}.module.ts/
src/modules/{feature}/{feature}.controller.ts/
src/modules/{feature}/{feature}.service.ts/
src/modules/{feature}/dto//
src/modules/{feature}/entities//
src/modules/{feature}/repositories//
src/common/guards//
src/common/filters//
src/common/interceptors//
src/common/pipes//
src/common/decorators//
src/config//
```

### Avoid
- Business logic in controllers
- Direct TypeORM/Prisma calls in services (use repositories)
- Circular module dependencies
- Hardcoded configuration values
- Using @Res() or @Next() in controllers — breaks interceptors and exception filters
- Global state outside the DI container
- Catching exceptions inside controllers instead of using filters
- Fat modules that own too many features

---
## Architectural Decisions
> **[rule]** Every business rule, calculation, and orchestration lives in a service. Services are @Injectable() and framework-agnostic — they never import @nestjs/common HTTP decorators or Express/Fastify types.
> **[rule]** Use @nestjs/event-emitter to decouple side effects (send email, invalidate cache, log analytics) from the main business operation. Emit events from services, handle them in dedicated listener classes.
> **[rule]** Use ConfigModule.forRoot() with a Joi or zod validation schema. The app must fail to start if required env vars are missing or have the wrong type. Never access process.env directly anywhere in the app.
> **[pattern]** Dynamic modules (database, cache) use forRoot() for static config and forRootAsync() when config depends on other providers like ConfigService.
> **[rule]** Version all public APIs from day one via URL prefix (/v1/) or header. Never make breaking changes to existing versions.
> **[rule]** Review and update dependencies at least monthly. Use automated tools (Dependabot, Renovate) to get PRs for new versions. Never ignore security advisories.
> **[rule]** Use UUIDs or slugs as public identifiers in API URLs and responses. Never expose sequential database integer IDs to clients.
> **[rule]** Sanitize all user-generated content before rendering. Escape HTML, strip scripts, and use CSP headers to prevent XSS.
> **[rule]** Group related config using registerAs(&quot;database&quot;, () &#x3D;&gt; ({ url: process.env.DATABASE_URL })). Inject with @InjectConfig(&quot;database&quot;) for typed access. Never use magic strings to access config keys.
> **[rule]** Services throw NotFoundException, ForbiddenException, BadRequestException, ConflictException — not raw Error objects. The global exception filter catches and formats them with the correct HTTP status automatically.
> **[rule]** Never use synchronize: true in any environment other than a throw-away local database. All schema changes are made through migrations generated via TypeORM CLI and committed to source control.
> **[rule]** Use soft deletes (deleted_at timestamp) for user-generated data. Hard delete only for PII removal on request.
> **[rule]** Attach a unique correlation/trace ID to every request. Propagate it through all downstream calls. Include it in logs and error responses.
> **[rule]** Use DTOs for all API responses. Never expose raw database models to the client.
> **[pattern]** Extend CreateUserDto with PartialType(CreateUserDto) to create UpdateUserDto. All fields become optional automatically with no duplication of validators.
> **[rule]** Controller methods never return TypeORM/Prisma entities. Always map to a response DTO before returning. Use plainToInstance(ResponseDto, entity) or a dedicated mapper class.
